description: An action that declares a counter variable and loops while a threshold condition holds, printing and/or decrementing it each iteration. Validated against the real transpiler across 20 variations (different action/variable names, start values, and thresholds), all parse/emit/compile/link/run clean. Supersedes an earlier hand-written version of this pattern that assumed the closer was `end repeat` -- real validated output always uses `end while`.

var.desc: loop counter variable name -- reused identically in the `keep`, `while`, `print`, and `set` lines. Never invent a different name partway through (this is the exact defect a real Cell 9-11 run produced: the model renamed the loop variable to match the action's own name instead of reusing the plan's variable).

init_value.desc: the starting value bound in the `keep` declaration

threshold.desc: the comparison value in the `while ... is greater than ...` condition

template:
action {action_name} produces nothing
    keep {var} as whole number with value {init_value}
    while {var} is greater than {threshold} repeat
        print the text {var} and newline
        set {var} to {var} minus 1
    end while
end action
requires: {var} must be declared via this pattern's own `keep` line -- it does not assume {var} already exists from an earlier chunk.
common_mistakes: Do not rename or lowercase {var} anywhere in the body -- `keep`, `while`, `print`, and `set` must all reference the exact same identifier.
The closing keyword is `end while`, not `end repeat`.
Do not add any statement after `end while` that wasn't part of the described loop body.
example:
action countdown produces nothing
    keep Count as whole number with value 5
    while Count is greater than 0 repeat
        print the text Count and newline
        set Count to Count minus 1
    end while
end action
